hypothesis: If a computational workflow integrating thermodynamic databases, cellular automaton simulation, machine learning descriptors, and commercial supplier databases is applied to inorganic material synthesis planning, then it will identify cost-effective and thermodynamically feasible synthesis recipes for target materials such as Ca2SiO4 more efficiently than traditional trial-and-error experimental approaches, because combining thermodynamic optimization with real-world economic constraints and predictive property modeling enables systematic prioritization of synthesis routes before any laboratory work is performed.
